task_completion.description: Open the 'Leaf' sample image in ImageJ/Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ/Fiji application is open and displays the 'Leaf' sample image.

task_completion.description: Calibrate the spatial scale to 10 cm for the image width
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Set Scale' dialog is visible, showing the distance in pixels (e.g., 507) mapped to a known distance of 10, with the unit of length set to 'cm'.

task_completion.description: Segment the leaf from the background
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image is shown with a threshold applied (e.g., a red overlay on the leaf or a black/white binary mask) separating the leaf structure from the background.

task_completion.description: Fill internal holes in the leaf mask to represent the solid leaf blade
task_completion.visual_evidence: The binary mask of the leaf is completely solid, with no internal gaps or holes (typically achieved via Process > Binary > Fill Holes).

task_completion.description: Measure the area and save the results to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An ImageJ 'Results' window shows the Area measurement (around 45-46 cm^2), and a 'Save As' dialog or file manager confirms the results are being saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/leaf_area.csv.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to perform the image processing and measurement
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show ImageJ menus, dialogs (like Set Scale, Threshold), and the Results table being actively used, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The measurement result comes from actual image processing, not hardcoding the expected value
integrity.visual_evidence: The Results table in ImageJ displays the calculated area alongside other default metrics, matching the visual state of the processed image, rather than a manually created CSV file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply create a text file with the expected area value
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) being used to manually type '45.92' into a CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Leaf (36K)' sample image is 507 pixels wide. The correct spatial calibration maps the full image width (507 pixels) to 10 cm. A correctly filled leaf mask (solid leaf blade with no internal holes) will yield an area measurement of approximately 45.92 cm^2 (acceptable range is > 45.0 cm^2). An unfilled mask (veins only) would measure significantly less (< 40.0 cm^2). The final result must be saved to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/leaf_area.csv.
